data_integrity.note: ZERO overlap verified between calibration and evaluation sets

dms_circuit_identification.note: d_head=64 dimensional (per-head, NOT d_model=896). High K because 0.5B model has weak/diffuse refusal circuits. Larger models expected to have sparser circuits.
dms_circuit_identification.granularity: TRUE per-head (verified: each refusal direction is d_head=64 dimensional)

safety_evaluation_held_out.methodology: Multi-seed evaluation on HELD-OUT prompts never seen during DMS calibration
safety_evaluation_held_out.judge: local heuristic (refusal prefix matching + compliance indicator detection)
safety_evaluation_held_out.interpretation: On this 0.5B model with only 6 held-out eval prompts, the confidence intervals are extremely wide and overlap heavily. The model's baseline refusal rate is already weak (ASR=0.44 vanilla) suggesting the 0.5B model has minimal safety alignment to begin with. The high circuit coverage (60% of heads) and very low delta threshold (0.000006) indicate the DMS is selecting nearly all heads because none have strong discriminative refusal signals. This is expected: a 0.5B instruction-tuned model has much weaker safety circuits than a 14B model.

reasoning_quality_in_process.methodology: In-process evaluation with steering hooks ACTIVE on same model object (NOT subprocess)

rad_cot_alpha_0.3.note: 23,250 corrections fired during 10 problems - confirming hooks ARE active

reasoning_quality_in_process.interpretation: Steering actually IMPROVED accuracy on this small benchmark (0.60 -> 0.80). This is likely noise from the tiny sample size (n=10), but critically demonstrates: (1) hooks are genuinely active during evaluation (23K corrections fired), (2) steering does not catastrophically break reasoning on this model.

what_this_validation_proves: 1. The per-head DMS pipeline runs end-to-end and produces true (layer, head) circuit indices
2. Refusal directions are d_head=64 dimensional (NOT d_model=896) - per-head granularity confirmed
3. Steering hooks actually fire during generation (23,250 corrections on 10 math problems)
4. Data split is enforced with ZERO overlap between calibration and evaluation
5. The entire pipeline is functional on real hardware with a real model
6. On a 0.5B model, safety circuits are weak and diffuse (K=202/336=60%), as expected
what_this_validation_does_NOT_prove: 1. ASR reduction claims from the paper (need 14B+ model with strong safety alignment)
2. Precise reasoning degradation numbers (need full GSM8K/MATH/HumanEval, not 10 arithmetic problems)
3. Generalization to unseen attacks (step 7 crashed before completing)
5. Any numbers should be cited with the actual confidence intervals, not as point estimates

recommendations_for_full_scale_validatio.model: Qwen3-14B or larger (strong safety alignment required)
recommendations_for_full_scale_validatio.eval_set_size: 156+ held-out AdvBench prompts (from 520 total with 70/30 split)
recommendations_for_full_scale_validatio.judge: GPT-4o with StrongREJECT rubric (not local heuristic)
recommendations_for_full_scale_validatio.reasoning_benchmarks: Full GSM8K (1319), MATH L4-5 (1324), HumanEval (164) via in-process eval
recommendations_for_full_scale_validatio.expected_circuit_sparsity: 15-40 heads out of 1600 (1-2.5%) based on stronger refusal signals
